scenarios.title: Tutoring Walkthrough
scenarios.summary: A calm preview of what tutoring feels like from the first click to walking out.

steps.title: You open the tutoring website.
steps.description: You land on the ASU tutoring page and see subject areas, tutoring options, and a simple menu for where to start.

steps.title: You pick your subject and a time slot.
steps.description: You choose the class you need help with and look at the option that fits your schedule, whether that is online, in person, or drop-in.

steps.title: You show up and the room is normal.
steps.description: There is a tutor, a couple of other students, and a regular study-space vibe. It feels more like working together than being put on the spot.

steps.title: The tutor asks what you are working on.
steps.description: You can show your homework, notes, or even say, 'I do not know where to start.' That is enough to begin.

steps.title: You work through it together.
steps.description: The tutor helps break the problem down. Nobody expects you to be polished or already understand everything.

steps.title: You leave. That is it.
steps.description: You got help, nothing dramatic happened, and it becomes much easier to imagine going back next time.

scenarios.title: Advising Walkthrough
scenarios.summary: What it feels like to sit down with an advisor when your schedule or degree path is confusing.

steps.title: You book the appointment in MyASU.
steps.description: The page asks what kind of help you need, and you choose the slot that works for your week.

steps.title: You arrive with questions, not perfect answers.
steps.description: It is normal to bring uncertainty: a confusing hold, class list, major question, or graduation concern.

steps.title: The advisor helps map the next move.
steps.description: They explain requirements in plain language and help you sort what matters now versus later.

steps.title: You leave with a plan.
steps.description: Usually that means a cleaner schedule, fewer unknowns, and a short list of next actions you can actually do.

scenarios.title: First Support Visit
scenarios.summary: A general first-day preview for coaching and other support spaces that can feel intimidating before you try them.

steps.title: You walk in and do not need a polished story.
steps.description: Most support spaces start with a simple check-in about what has been hard lately.

steps.title: Someone helps narrow the problem.
steps.description: Instead of solving your whole semester at once, you focus on the next useful step.

steps.title: They connect you to the right resource.
steps.description: That might be coaching, tutoring, financial aid, counseling, or an advisor depending on what you need.

steps.title: You leave knowing what happens next.
steps.description: The biggest change is usually clarity: what to click, who to email, where to go, and why it is worth trying.
